back_translation: For the choirmaster. To the tune of 'The Lilies.' A Maskil of the sons of Korah. A love song.
My heart overflows with a good theme; I address my verses to the king; my tongue is the pen of a skillful scribe.
You are the most handsome of the sons of men; grace flows upon your lips; therefore God has blessed you forever.
O mighty one, gird your sword at your side, in your splendor and your majesty.
In your majesty ride forth victoriously for the cause of truth, humility, and justice; let your right hand show awesome deeds.
Your arrows are sharp, piercing the hearts of the king's enemies; the peoples fall beneath you.
Your throne, O God, endures forever and ever, and the scepter of Your kingdom is a scepter of justice.
You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of joy above your companions.
All your garments are fragrant with myrrh, aloes, and cassia; from palaces of ivory the harps make you glad.
The daughters of kings are among your honored women; the queen stands at your right hand, adorned with the gold of Ophir.
Listen, O daughter, consider and incline your ear; forget your people and your father's house,
and the king will desire your beauty; for he is your lord, bow down to him.
The Daughter of Tyre will come with a gift; the wealthy among the people will seek your favor.
All glorious is the king's daughter within her chamber; her gown is woven with gold.
She is led to the king in embroidered garments; the virgin companions who follow her are brought to you.
They are led in with joy and gladness; they enter the palace of the king.
Your sons will succeed your fathers; you will make them princes throughout the land.
I will make your name remembered through all generations; therefore the peoples will praise you forever and ever.
